You are a project manager for the Apache Kafka project.

A developer has raised this issue: "Add metrics for consumer lag monitoring with Prometheus integration"
Domain: monitoring | Complexity: medium

Break this down into a well-structured task with full project management detail.

Return JSON with this exact structure:
{
  "task_id": "TASK-XXX",
  "title": "short action-verb title",
  "description": "2-3 sentence detailed description",
  "acceptance_criteria": ["criterion 1", "criterion 2", "criterion 3"],
  "story_points": <fibonacci: 1,2,3,5,8,13>,
  "priority": "Critical|High|Medium|Low",
  "labels": ["label1", "label2"],
  "dependencies": ["describe any blocking tasks or leave empty array"],
  "definition_of_done": "clear measurable done state"
}

{
  "task_id": "TASK-042",
  "title": "Implement Prometheus consumer‑lag metrics",
  "description": "Add a new set of Prometheus‑compatible metrics that expose per‑partition consumer lag for Kafka consumers. The implementation should instrument the existing consumer fetch loop, expose the metrics via the existing JMX‑to‑Prometheus bridge, and include configuration options to enable or disable the metrics at runtime.",
  "acceptance_criteria": [
    "Consumer lag metrics are exported with the prefix kafka_consumer_lag and include labels for consumer_group, topic, and partition.",
    "Metrics are visible in Prometheus after enabling the new configuration flag and can be queried without affecting consumer performance.",
    "Unit and integration tests verify correct metric values under normal and edge‑case consumption patterns."
  ],
  "story_points": 8,
  "priority": "High",
  "labels": [
    "monitoring",
    "metrics",
    "prometheus",
    "kafka"
  ],
  "dependencies": [],
  "definition_of_done": "Code changes merged into the main branch, all tests passing, documentation updated with usage examples, and the feature verified in a staging environment with Prometheus scraping the new metrics."
}

{

  "title": "Implement Search and Filtering",
  "description": "Develop a searchable interface for the SaaS platform allowing users to query patient records and filter results by date, status, and provider. Integrate backend API endpoints with frontend components, ensuring performance, security, and a consistent user experience.",
  "acceptance_criteria": [
    "Search returns relevant results within 2 seconds for a dataset of 10,000 records",
    "Filters can be applied individually and in any combination, and the UI reflects active filters",
    "All user inputs are validated and sanitized to prevent injection attacks",
    "Unit tests cover at least 80% of the new code and integration tests verify end‑to‑end functionality",

    "search",
    "frontend",
    "backend"
  ],
  "dependencies": [],
